title: Pleopeltis variabilis
taxonomy: Pleopeltis variabilis is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is also known as the variable polypody or variable chain fern. Pleopeltis variabilis is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. The classification of Pleopeltis variabilis is further divided into several varieties, which can be distinguished by their leaf morphology and growth habits. As a member of the genus Pleopeltis, it is closely related to other species of the genus, particularly Pleopeltis gynurus.
uses: Pleopeltis variabilis, also known as the variable polypody, is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are used to treat various health issues. In traditional medicine, Pleopeltis variabilis is used to treat wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. The plant's leaves are also used to make teas and infusions that are believed to have medicinal properties. More research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and potential side effects.
